Title: Federal Territories PKR Youth chief quits party
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories PKR Youth chief has quit the party, citing the party's drift from the reform principles he had believed in since his days as an activist.
"After more than half a term of governing the country, I see this party moving further away from the foundations of the fight I believe in," said Asheeq Ali Sethi Alivi in his announcement on Facebook today.
Asheeq, who is also PKR Batu branch chief, said he had joined PKR shortly after the Pakatan Harapan (PH) government was toppled through the Sheraton Move in 2020, believing at the time that the party needed the energy and idealism of young people and was the best vehicle for change in Malaysian politics.
He added that he had initially believed there was still room to reconcile himself with the direction the party was taking, but found himself increasingly caught between his idealism and what he described as a growing culture of pragmatism within PKR.
"Many decisions made by the government today deviate from the foundations of the struggle I have believed in since my days in activism until now," he said.
He announced his resignation from all party positions effective immediately.
He also said he would serve a two-week notice to the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh regarding his resignation as Residents Representative Council, and Federal Territories (MPPWP) chairman for the Batu parliamentary constituency.
Asheeq said that his departure from the party would not affect his commitment to the Batu community.
"Whatever my political decisions after this, I will continue my work and service in Batu as a local son born here.
"My commitment to the community has never been tied to any position or party alone," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to teachers: Stay positive in face of criticism
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on educators to remain resilient in the face of criticism, saying such challenges should be viewed constructively to strengthen the teaching profession and uplift future generations.
He said teachers must continue to play a central role in shaping students' character and values, despite being increasingly affected by social issues that spill into schools.
"Teachers appear to be burdened. There are always issues. Social problems inevitably affect students in schools and naturally cause disruption.
"Many of these challenges reflect broader moral and social weaknesses within society," he said in his speech at the Teachers' Day celebration themed Exemplary Teachers, Heartbeat of the Nation at a hotel here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Anwar said that education cannot be separated from the formation of character and human values, noting that the ultimate purpose of schooling goes beyond academic achievement.
"Education is about seeking knowledge, improving understanding, evaluating well, and shaping human beings with values and ethics.
"But this is not happening sufficiently in society, politics, or even social media," he said.
He also acknowledged the pressures faced by teachers, who often become the first point of contact for societal problems affecting students.
"If not teachers, then who else?" he said, calling on educators to continue their role positively even when confronted with unfair criticism.
Anwar added that the education system must balance technological advancement with the preservation of human values, warning against allowing hatred or racial prejudice to take root in society.
He also said it is important to embrace diverse civilisational values, including Confucian and Indian philosophical traditions, alongside Islamic teachings, which he said form the moral foundation of the nation.
"There is no place in education for narrow thinking. In education, we honour all perspectives," he said, adding that mutual respect is key to earning respect from others.
At the event, Anwar presented awards to SK Putrajaya Presint 11(1), which has been named the winner of the World's Best School Prize 2025 for Supporting Healthy Lives; the National Educational Leadership Figure Award to Dr Ahmad Rafee Che Kassim; and the National Teacher Figure Award to Abdul Rasid Bakar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Subsidy reforms not strictly reliant on income classification, says Economy Minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government does not strictly rely on income classifications such as T20 when formulating subsidy policies, saying a broader and more holistic approach is needed in ongoing discussions on subsidy targeting.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry avoids rigid classifications such as "T20", as household income alone does not fully reflect economic reality.
"I have never really used terms like T or this or T that.
"From the Economy Ministry's perspective, it is more relevant to look at things comprehensively because economic comparisons are not just about income, but also essential expenditure and other factors," he told reporters here today.
Akmal said subsidy rationalisation remains part of the government's wider economic restructuring agenda aimed at ensuring assistance is delivered more effectively to the intended groups.
He said the matter is regularly reviewed under both long-term planning, including the 13th Malaysia Plan, and short-term responses to economic pressures.
"From the perspective of the Economy Ministry, what we are trying to achieve, whether through the 13th Malaysia Plan or when facing economic challenges, is the need to restructure the economy into a better state to ensure continuity and resilience."
Akmal said discussions on more targeted subsidy distribution are ongoing, particularly to ensure government programmes reach their intended beneficiaries.
"There are always discussions on making subsidies more targeted because we want programmes and contributions to reach the right groups.
"The challenge is determining the threshold or criteria that can be used."
He said the government has already announced plans to implement subsidy reforms for diesel as part of a broader effort to ensure fiscal sustainability and better targeting.
He said the approach was necessary given ongoing global uncertainties and rising cost pressures.
"We are structuring this in stages so that when we roll it out, we do so in a way that manages rising pressures. We know that as long as global conflicts persist, the burden will continue to grow."
He added that mitigation measures and policy interventions would continue to be introduced from time to time to manage economic challenges.
Critics
have previously argued that the move unfairly paints all T20 earners as financially comfortable, despite many grappling with high living costs, housing commitments, childcare expenses and mounting financial obligations in major cities.
"The best policy is not one that punishes income but one that reduces waste, protects necessity, discourages excess and preserves public trust," said Samirul Ariff Othman, a senior consultant at Global Asia Consulting.
He had said that targeting the T20 risks oversimplifying a highly diverse group while creating the perception that higher earners are being unfairly penalised despite already contributing disproportionately through income taxes.
This follows the Putrajaya plan to consider excluding the T20 income group from the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) fuel subsidy scheme.
Akmal also previously said the government is currently
prioritising
the rollout of the Budi diesel programme.
He said there has been no discussion on any reduction in the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) quota in the National Economic Action Council.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Honouring teachers as the pulse of the nation's future
Article:
LETTERS:
In conjunction with the 2026 Teachers' Day celebration, themed "Guru Bitara Nadi Negara" (Teacher Champions, the Nation's Pulse), we extend our highest appreciation and heartfelt gratitude to all educators across the country for their immeasurable dedication, iron commitment and selfless sacrifices to forge the very soul of Malaysia's future.
This year's theme is more than a mere symbolic expression - it represents a profound recognition of teachers as the driving force behind national development and societal progress.
The role of teachers as catalysts for change and agents of social transformation requires continuous support from all stakeholders.
To sustain and amplify their monumental impact, unwavering and comprehensive support must flow from every corner of the society, from the Ministry of Education (MOE), policymakers to educational institutions and every citizen who cherishes Malaysia's growth and future.
In the era of the Fourth Industrial Revolution and the transition towards a digital economy and artificial intelligence (AI), our national education system faces an urgent need to adapt rapidly to technological advancements and evolving global market demands.
Today's teachers are expected to be more than traditional educators - they must possess technological proficiency, understand the dynamics of digital learning and be capable of fearlessly integrating cutting-edge innovation into every dimension of their teaching.
A key challenge lies in ensuring that the emphasis on future-ready skills does not come at the expense of foundational education, including literacy, numeracy and values-based learning.
There is a real risk that the future-ready agenda may become overly rhetorical if foundational learning disparities, particularly between urban and rural communities, are not addressed systematically.
The Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035, launched by the MOE in January 2026, positions teachers as the primary drivers of national education transformation.
But, long-standing structural issues such as excessive non-academic workloads, assessment pressures and limited opportunities for continuous professional development must be resolved.
Teachers' wellbeing remains a national priority. Findings by the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) indicate that mental health concerns among teachers demand sustained, urgent attention.
As such, work-life balance and mental wellbeing must be placed at the forefront of the ministry's agenda as educators who are emotionally, mentally and physically resilient are better equipped to fulfil their responsibilities effectively.
In this regard, MASA urges the MOE to undertake more proactive and systematic measures to strengthen the dignity and standing of the teaching profession through improved access to modern educational infrastructure and technology, continuous competency development opportunities and appropriate recognition for their contributions.
MASA wishes all educators a meaningful and inspiring Teachers' Day celebration. Thank you for your unwavering service, sacrifices and dedication in nurturing the champions and visionaries of tomorrow.
You are not only teachers – you are the unshakeable backbone, the blazing torch and heartbeat of national development.
AZRIL MOHD AMIN
Chief Executive Officer
Institut Masa Depan Malaysia (MASA)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More retention ponds to be built across KL to curb flash floods
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is stepping up efforts to reduce flooding by building on-site retention ponds across selected green spaces in the city.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said DBKL has already begun constructing retention ponds at several sites and is identifying additional locations for new ponds.
"DBKL is building on-site retention ponds to increase the number of ponds at sites such as the Botanical Gardens and Padang Merbok.
"I have asked the mayor and the Land Office to identify more sites where we can create on-site retention ponds," she said.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five siblings follow family legacy in teaching profession
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A family of educators from rural Sarawak reflects the spirit of this year's Teachers' Day theme, "Guru Bitara, Nadi Negara", with teaching serving not just as a profession but as a legacy carried on by almost all the siblings.
SK Sebako senior assistant for student affairs Mohamad Azwan Azahari said his parents, who each served as teachers for more than 30 years, were the main inspiration behind the siblings' interest in education from a young age.
The eldest of five siblings said his father is from Kampung Sebako, while his mother is from Kampung Bruit, both villages located on Pulau Bruit in Daro, Sarawak.
Azwan has been in the teaching profession for 10 years, currently teaching at his hometown school, while two of his younger siblings are teachers at SMK Beladin and SK Bandar Sungai Buaya in Selangor, respectively.
Another sibling, who graduated in economics from Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (Unimas), also intends to pursue a diploma in education, while the youngest is currently studying at the Teachers Education Institute, Tun Abdul Razak Campus.
"When we were young, our parents would often bring students home to our quarters for extra classes. They even put up a blackboard on the wall so lessons could be conducted there.
"What stands out most is that many of their former students still keep in touch with my parents and treat them with kindness even now. I believe much of the good our family enjoys today also comes, in part, from their prayers," he said.
Azwan, 34, said the family quarters often become a gathering place for pupils, especially on weekends.
"My mother always reminded us to teach and care for students with the same love and kindness we would want for our own children.
"She would also often say that we should work sincerely, because only then will our salary be truly blessed," he said.
Azwan said one of the sacrifices teachers often make, but which is frequently overlooked, is the time, money, energy, and attention they devote to their students compared with their own families, to ensure students receive the best possible education.
"We as children of teachers could only spend time with our parents during school holidays," he said, adding that this taught them to be independent from a young age, although there were times when they felt disappointed that their parents spent more time with other people's children.
"However, when I became a teacher myself, I began to understand. At times, teachers become a source of comfort for their students, who turn to them for affection, companionship, laughter and conversation.
"Perhaps the atmosphere at home is not as warm as it is at school or in the classroom," he said.
Reflecting on the challenges of being an educator, Azwan said he had at times experienced emotional burnout when students failed to meet the expected learning targets, adding that he would often reflect on his own performance and sometimes feel that he was not good enough as a teacher.
"Perhaps I was also putting too much pressure on myself by conducting extra classes on weekends and during school holidays, leaving myself with too little rest.
"However, seeing students who are eager to learn and constantly curious encourages me to pick myself up again and find more suitable teaching methods that fit their level and learning styles," he said.
Azwan also expressed his gratitude to all his former teachers, lecturers and those who had guided him along the way, helping him become an educator today.
"Our teaching career may not make us rich in material wealth, but it is rich in the prayers of our students. May we always be granted the strength and good health to continue educating the nation's children," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspected drunk driver in Klang wrong-way crash to be charged Monday
Article:
KLANG: A suspected drunk driver who drove against traffic and crashed into a motorcyclist, leaving the victim in critical condition, will be charged at the magistrate's court here on Monday.
South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said the investigation paper into the incident had been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor's office for further action.
"The driver will be charged on Monday at the Klang magistrate's court," he said when contacted today.
It was reported yesterday that the
remand
of the man, in his 30s, who allegedly drove against traffic before crashing into a motorcycle along Persiaran Kota, has been extended by four days until next Tuesday.
The victim, a man in his 20s, remains in critical condition at the intensive care unit
of Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital.
The suspect had no prior criminal or drug records.
It was previously reported that the
motorcyclist was hit
by a car driven by a man believed to be intoxicated and travelling against traffic about 12.30am.
The victim was thrown from his motorcycle upon impact, suffering severe injuries to his body and right leg.
Initial investigations found the suspect had been driving from Port Klang towards Shah Alam before allegedly veering into the opposite lane and travelling against traffic for about 3.5km.
Screening tests confirmed the driver was positive for alcohol.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yeoh to meet MACC for more details on KL retention pond issue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh wants to meet the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over alleged irregularities involving the alienation of the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds.
Yeoh said she wants to obtain more information on the matter before drawing any conclusions on the case.
She said that although the MACC has denied any element of corruption in the transaction, she does not want to rely solely on its report and will arrange a meeting this week to obtain further details.
"Just because the MACC has said there is no corruption involved, I don't want to rely solely on its report.
"I want a meeting with the MACC to find out more details," she said after launching the KL Free of Smoke community programme in Gombak.
She also criticised the land transactions, saying they had resulted in a reduction of green space.
"Even if something has no element of corruption, that does not mean the transaction is a good one.
"The decision to give land to a developer and to reduce the waterbody by 70 per cent was an unwise decision. I can say this was a foolish decision."
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economy minister: Rafizi's vending machine project helped the B40
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Economy Ministry today defended the People's Income Initiative – Food Entrepreneur Initiative (IPR-INSAN) vending machine programme, saying the initiative has shown proven success in boosting the income of poor and asnaf (underprivileged) participants.
This comes despite calls for an Auditor-General's probe into the project championed by Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry's focus remained on ensuring the programme delivered tangible results for targeted groups rather than responding to external speculation.
"That is why, in terms of programmes under the Economy Ministry, our approach is to ensure the best possible implementation.
"As for reports or calls for investigations, those are beyond our scope to control. But at the same time, when we look at the actual results on the ground, as I mentioned earlier, we have personally met participants in Kedah, Johor and Kuala Lumpur who benefited from the initiative.
"This proves that there are groups we targeted, especially the poor and asnaf communities, who have successfully increased their income," he told reporters here today.
Akmal said the ministry's priority for 2026 would remain focused on implementation and ensuring assistance reached those genuinely in need.
"So the spirit at the Economy Ministry is that this programme exists purely to help people who truly need assistance, while issues raised outside are subject to individual interpretations," he said.
Previously, Auditor-General Datuk Seri Wan Suraya Wan Mohd Radzi was called on to investigate the status of the nasi lemak vending machine project spearheaded by Rafizi when he was economy minister.
Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi said the investigation was necessary to determine whether the project remained effective, whether there was any wastage involved, or whether there had been inflated procurement costs for the vending machines.
The vending machine project under the People's Income Initiative – Food Entrepreneur Initiative (IPR-INSAN), which sells nasi lemak and other food items, was introduced on Feb 26, 2023.
The initiative was launched by the Economy Ministry to help B40 groups generate additional income, with the first 100 machines beginning operations in June 2023.
PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan had also criticised the project, valued at RM750 million, during the Johor PKR Convention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Government boosts asnaf economy via Economy Ministry, MAIWP collaboration
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asnaf groups will continue to remain a priority under the People's Income Initiative (IPR) despite current economic challenges and concerns over government spending, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said.
The programme, he said, was specifically designed to assist low-income groups, particularly amid increasingly challenging economic conditions.
"These are the groups we are targeting the most.
"These are the people we should help, especially when the economic situation is becoming more challenging," he said to reporters here after attending the launching ceremony of a strategic collaboration between the Economy Ministry and the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP).
The strategic collaboration, through the People's Income Initiative – Food Entrepreneur Initiative (IPR-INSAN), aims to strengthen the economic standing of asnaf groups and small entrepreneurs in the Federal Territories.
Under the collaboration, the Economy Ministry will provide food vending machines and fully subsidise rental costs for 24 months, while MAIWP will provide start-up capital assistance to eligible asnaf entrepreneur participants, subject to stipulated conditions and standard operating procedures, to help them generate sustainable income.
Akmal said the costs involved mainly covered the provision of machines and rental expenses, adding that discussions were ongoing with MAIWP on the matter.
He said the Economy Ministry might not even need to fully absorb the rental costs, as the initiative was aimed at enabling participants to generate sustainable income rather than merely relying on aid.
"The value generated is far more beneficial. In terms of returns compared to the rental paid, the ratio is about 2.6 times higher."
Akmal said the programme had generated RM20.46 million in earnings, while the rental costs borne by the government amounted to RM7 million to RM8 million.
He added that the initiative also benefits surrounding communities by offering meals at affordable prices, with food sold for less than RM5.
"Some programme participants have also managed to record monthly sales of between RM4,000 and RM10,000 through the placement of vending machines at strategic locations such as MRT stations, Urban Transformation Centres (UTCs) and other public hotspots.
"The implementation of IPR-INSAN has shown positive and measurable results. As of May 5, 2026, a total of 1,125 vending machines had been installed nationwide, with 712 active participants recording cumulative sales exceeding RM2,000 since operations began."
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the synergy between MAIWP and the Economy Ministry reflected the government's commitment towards holistic community development through a whole-of-government approach.
Zulkifli said MAIWP was not merely distributing zakat assistance routinely, but was also aiming to produce more self-reliant asnaf entrepreneurs capable of escaping poverty.
"At the same time, Masjid Wilayah Persekutuan will also serve as a new location for the placement of vending machines as part of efforts to strengthen the role of mosques not only as places of worship, but also as hubs for the ummah economy."
As of May 15, 2026, MAIWP had distributed RM449.84 million in zakat funds through 38 aid schemes, in addition to indirect assistance via asnaf development programmes and MAIWP educational institutions.
Zulkifli added that MAIWP hoped the initiative would produce more successful entrepreneurs from among the asnaf community who could eventually become zakat contributors in the future.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuziah: PKR MP's call for ministers to quit over UEC a 'personal opinion'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh says Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim's call for the higher education and education ministers to resign over the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) issue is his personal opinion.
She said the issue was not new, as previous governments had also accepted the certificate under specific conditions, including candidates having a Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification with passes in Bahasa Melayu and History.
She added that the matter had been accepted by other parties in the past and should not be politicised.
"That is his (Hassan's) personal view. Actually, this UEC issue is not a new issue. Other parties also recognised it, so there is no issue. That is his personal view," she said.
Yesterday, Hassan, who is PKR's Pasir Gudang member of parliament, called on Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir and Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek to step down for allegedly failing to defend the national education policy.
This came after the Higher Education Ministry announced that school leavers from tahfiz institutions and UEC holders can pursue specific programmes at public universities.
The move was agreed upon following a cabinet decision on the entry pathways into public universities for students from outside the national education system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: From used pencil and pen, gifts that still move retired teachers' hearts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Years after leaving the classroom, three retired teachers still find themselves revisiting moments from their teaching journeys, including heartfelt gestures from their students during Teachers' Day celebrations.
To them, it was never about the value of the gifts received but the sincerity behind them.
Even the most modest presents, they said, carried meanings far greater than their material worth.
Muhammad Mustafa Abdul Salam, 58, said the most touching and unforgettable gift he ever received was a used pencil and eraser.
"The student wrote that he could not afford to buy an expensive gift.
"But he wanted the pencil and eraser to serve as a token of a student's appreciation for his teacher.
"I was overwhelmed and brought to tears after reading the note," he said.
Moved by his student's sincerity, Mustafa said he went on to buy the student new school supplies.
He has kept the used pencil and eraser to this day as his most cherished memory from 30 years of teaching at SK Taman Megah, SMK Assunta, and SMK Lembah Subang.
For P. Puvana, 63, tears still well up whenever she recalls receiving a sari as a gift.
"My students pooled their money together. They were willing to do that just to appreciate me.
"I never expected such a gesture," she said.
Nazri Zainun, 62, said he still keeps a Parker pen gifted by a student from a modest rural family.
"That pen is very meaningful. I know he put in great effort to give me the best gift, even though his family was not well-off.
"I still keep it as a memory," he said.
All three retired teachers see Teachers' Day as a reminder that the sacrifices of teachers are priceless.
Small gifts from students remain lasting proof of love that is most precious to an educator.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says ISIS second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki eliminated
Article:
BENGALURU: US President Donald Trump said on Friday that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second-in-command of ISIS globally, had been eliminated in an operation conducted by US and Nigerian forces.
"Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the armed forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield.
Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second-in-command of ISIS globally, thought he could hide in Africa, but little did he know we had sources who kept us informed on what he was doing," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
He also thanked the Nigerian government for its partnership in the operation.
Nigeria had earlier come under scrutiny from Trump, who had said that Christians there were being persecuted, which the African nation's government denies.
The US had struck what it said were Islamist bases in northwestern Nigeria on Christmas Day last year, following Trump's accusations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL demolishes illegal food stall in Cheras after owner ignores eight-month notice
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: City hall demolished an unauthorised business structure in Cheras on Thursday after its owner failed to comply with enforcement notices issued nearly eight months ago.
In a post on Facebook, DBKL said the structure at Jalan Senohong, Taman Bukit Ria, which was used for food business operations, was torn down on May 14 following the owner's failure to act on an enforcement and site vacation notice issued on Sept 23 last year and a follow-up visit on Oct 7.
The action was taken under the Street, Drainage and Building Act.
DBKL said the demolition and site clearance proceeded smoothly after the owner removed all goods and equipment from the premises in accordance with standard operating procedures prior to the operation.
Members of the public can lodge complaints or submit information at https://adukl.dbkl.gov.my.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia must make clear Jho Low should face justice, says Gobind
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP chairman Gobind Singh Deo says Malaysia must make clear that fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, should face justice over the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal, amid reports of his bid for a presidential pardon in the United States.
Gobind said while he accepted Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's view that the
pardon was entirely for the US government to decide
, Malaysia must still make its stance clear.
"All those involved and responsible in cases around the 1MDB scandal must face action without exception," he said in a statement.
Gobind, who is also the digital minister, said he would raise the matter in the next cabinet meeting on Wednesday.
He said the country must remain consistent in its position that all those responsible for the multi-billion ringgit financial scandal face justice.
"It took an entire nation to remove the government in 2018 to bring those responsible for it to justice.
"We cannot, and must never allow, the architects of this crime and those involved in enabling it to walk free, bypassing the doors of justice," he said.
Gobind said any action against Low in the US must be seen as an integral part of the broader accountability process.
"Those responsible in this global conspiracy must be brought to justice for crimes committed in connection with it, whether it be here in Malaysia or anywhere else in the world where events, more so events criminal in nature, took place," he said.
Gobind also said Low must face charges in Malaysia regardless of developments abroad.
Anwar yesterday said the reported application for a pardon from US President Donald Trump by Low rested solely with the US government, adding that Malaysia would not intervene.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil previously said the cabinet was expected to
discuss the matter
next week.
Low, described as the mastermind behind the 1MDB scandal, recently filed a
request for a pardon
that, if granted, would remove criminal charges against him in the US.
A White House official reportedly said Low's request was not currently on its radar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Workload cut: Manual co-curricular record-keeping for schools abolished
Article:
IPOH: The Education Ministry has abolished manual co-curricular record-keeping in a move aimed at reducing teachers' administrative workload and allowing them to focus more on teaching and students.
Minister Fadhlina Sidek announced this at the national Teacher's Day celebration, which was also attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at a hotel here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Fadhlina said schools would be given autonomy to manage co-curricular records before entering students' data into the Physical, Sports and Co-Curricular Assessment System.
She said the ministry had also instructed that the system be integrated with the Student Database Application system to streamline management and improve efficiency.
"Our commitment is to ensure that reducing teachers' workload remains a priority. We want teachers to focus on educational initiatives, classrooms and students.
"With this, the manual co-curricular recording system is abolished. Schools will be given autonomy to manage co-curricular records before entering students' information into the system," she said.
Fadhlina said integrating the Physical, Sports and Co-Curricular Assessment System with the student database application would further enhance systematic management in schools.
"When one system is removed, teachers can focus more on classrooms and students," she added.
The announcement was among several initiatives unveiled by the ministry in conjunction with Teachers' Day today, focusing on teachers' welfare, professional development and digital empowerment.
Among the key measures announced was the continuation of free Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level 1 and Level 2 certification programmes for teachers nationwide.
Fadhlina said Malaysia is currently recognised as the country with the highest number of Google-certified educators globally, with 60,000 GCE Level 1 holders and 33,000 GCE Level 2 holders.
"This proves that our teachers have never been left behind in demonstrating excellence, competency and mastery in technology," she said.
She added that the ministry has also introduced a special Federal Training Award (HLP) for teachers serving in hardship schools, including hospital schools, floating schools, and schools in remote P1, P2 and P3 areas.
Under the initiative, selected teachers will be given opportunities to pursue Master's and PhD studies.
Fadhlina said the award recognises the sacrifices and resilience of teachers serving in challenging environments.
She said the ministry announced an allocation of RM169 million for the supply of devices to schools nationwide as part of preparations for the 2027 school curriculum.
She said the initiative was aimed at strengthening the use of digital technology in teaching and learning.
"An additional RM100 million was also allocated for the maintenance and upgrading of teachers' rooms, on top of RM100 million previously announced under the Malaysia Education Plan.
"The allocation will cover improvements such as air-conditioning units, printers, and other facilities to enhance teachers' comfort and well-being.
"At the same time, RM50 million has been allocated for the maintenance of teachers' quarters to ensure more comfortable and conducive accommodation," she said.
She also announced RM122.4 million in direct allocations to schools for STEM and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes under the 2027 curriculum.
Fadhlina said the funds would be channelled directly to schools to enable teachers to organise programmes at school level.
In conjunction with Teachers' Day, Fadhlina said the Tun Hussein Onn Teacher Foundation will provide RM1,000 to teachers whose children scored 10As in the 2025 SPM examination.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly sisters killed, teen injured in hit-and-run
Article:
SHAH ALAM: An elderly woman and her sister, both pedestrians, were killed while another person was injured in a hit-and-run at Jalan Pekan Baru, Bandar Baru Klang, yesterday.
North Klang district police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the two women were aged 57 and 62, while the injured victim is a 17-year-old boy, who is the son of one of the women.
He said the North Klang district police headquarters' Traffic Complaint Counter received information on the accident at 8.14pm.
He added that preliminary investigations showed the incident occurred about 7.40pm when the driver of a silver sports utility vehicle (SUV), travelling from the direction of Jalan Meru towards Bandar Baru Klang, was believed to have lost control of the vehicle before crashing into three pedestrians on the left shoulder of the road.
"The driver of the vehicle then fled the scene.
"The two female victims succumbed to severe head injuries and died at the scene, while another victim sustained minor injuries.
"He is currently receiving treatment at Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital, Klang," he said in a statement today.
He said the three family members were believed to have been travelling from their home in the same area for dinner nearby.
He added that police have yet to record a statement from the injured teenager.
"Members of the public who have information regarding the incident are urged to contact the Traffic Investigating Officer, Inspector Ahmad Fadzli Abdullah at 017-5843860 or report to any nearby police station.
"The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: One-way ticket home: 28 foreign scammers to be deported
Article:
TAWAU: Twenty-eight foreigners convicted of involvement in "love" and online investment scams will be deported by the Immigration Department.
The foreigners, comprising 27 Chinese nationals, including a woman and a Vietnamese woman aged between 21 and 59, pleaded guilty to charges of criminal conspiracy in online fraud on Thursday.
They were fined RM5,000 each in default of up to four months in jail.
Tawau police chief Assistant Commissioner Jasmin Hussin said the suspects were arrested during a raid on four luxury bungalows on May 6.
He said the syndicate conducted online scams targeting South Koreans via WhatsApp and Kakao Talk.
"They were charged under Section 120B(2) of the Penal Code," he said in a statement, adding all the suspects had settled their fines.
Jasmin said the foreigners were then handed over to the Immigration Department for deportation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police to boost sensitivity training for handling autism cases
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bukit Aman wants society to move beyond simply recognising autism and instead embrace greater acceptance, understanding and inclusivity for people on the spectrum.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, in his address during the Autism Day celebration, said this year's theme, "Acceptance, Understanding, Inclusivity", reflected the need for Malaysians to better appreciate the perspectives and experiences of autistic people.
"Autism is not a deficiency that needs to be cured, but rather a unique variation of human individuality," he said.
He said the police force remained committed to supporting the autism community through inclusive initiatives, awareness efforts and continued backing for the Pengaman-PDRM Autism Centre.
Khalid said the centre's establishment in August 2020 also demonstrated that the responsibilities of the police extend beyond law enforcement duties.
"We remain committed to advancing social inclusivity by providing a conducive ecosystem for autistic children and their families.
"The holistic approach implemented at the centre, from early intervention to emotional support, is key to helping these special children reach their fullest potential and contribute meaningfully to society in the future," he said.
He added that Bukit Aman would continue strengthening sensitivity training among officers and personnel to ensure interactions with autistic people were handled professionally, compassionately and respectfully.
Khalid, however, stressed that support for the autism community required collective responsibility and could not be undertaken by the police alone.
He called for closer cooperation between the police, Yayasan Pengaman Malaysia, government agencies and non-governmental organisations to further strengthen support systems for autistic children and their families.
Khalid said he hopes that the Pengaman-PDRM Autism Centre would one day emerge as a leading regional centre for autism intervention.
"With multidisciplinary expertise available, the centre is capable of setting new standards in helping autistic individuals become independent, capable and competitive," he said.
He also encouraged everyone to continue contributing time, effort and resources towards building a more inclusive society.
"We must not allow anyone to be marginalised or left behind in the nation's development," he added.
Khalid also thanked donors, corporate bodies and those who had contributed financially and materially towards sustaining the centre's operations.
Meanwhile, autism centre committee chairman Tan Sri Hussin Ismail said the centre's growth since its establishment in 2020 was made possible through support from various parties, particularly the police force and members of the public.
"The support shown is an impetus for us to increase the quality of early intervention at an affordable cost," he said.
Centre manager Azura Izham said the facility began operations in 2020 with only six autistic children and eight personnel.
"Now we are able to cater to 50 children with 18 dedicated personnel," she said.
Azura said autistic children should not be pressured to change to fit societal expectations, but instead deserved to be understood based on how they viewed and experienced the world.
"Their success may not be the same as everyone else's, but every small step they take is a victory for them and their parents," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Female bus driver loses RM537,000 in online stock investment scam
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A 48-year-old bus driver lost more than RM500,000 after falling victim to an online stock investment scam.
Seri Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Sohaimi Ishak said the woman claimed to have been cheated after being deceived by an offer on Facebook promising double profits.
He said the incident began in March when the victim saw a stock investment advertisement on the social media platform and clicked the provided link.
"The victim was promised high profits in a short period. She then conducted 12 transactions into two different bank accounts from March 5 to May 8, totalling RM537,201," he said in a statement today.
The victim was later asked to make additional payments to withdraw the investment profits but failed to receive any returns.
Sohaimi said the woman realised she had been cheated when efforts to recover her money failed.
She lodged a report yesterday and the case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating.
"The investigation will also cover the involvement of 'mule accounts', and action will be taken against the account owners," he said.
He advised the public not to be easily deceived by any investment promising high monetary returns.
Sohaimi also reminded the public to remain cautious and check the legitimacy of companies through the Securities Commission Malaysia to avoid becoming victims of scams.
He said the public should avoid providing personal or banking information to unknown parties and not be deceived by promises of large profits in a short time.
"Use the Semak Mule portal to check suspicious accounts before conducting transactions.
"The public is also urged to follow the social media pages of the Commercial Crime Investigation Department to learn the latest modi operandi of commercial crimes," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar on UEC: From refusing to speak Malay 40 years ago, Chinese school mindsets have shifted
Article:
IPOH: The government's latest decision involving Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) students was made possible following significant changes in attitudes and acceptance over the past four decades, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said discussions on recognising UEC graduates first began during his tenure as education minister about 40 years ago, when Chinese independent schools were largely detached from the national education system.
He recalled that meetings between education authorities and Chinese education groups at the time were often tense, with some representatives refusing to speak in Bahasa Melayu.
"During several meetings I attended, the leaders of those teachers' associations refused to speak even a single word in Bahasa Melayu.
"I remained patient and listened, but the then director-general of education, Tan Sri Rahman Arshad, whispered to me: 'Anwar, please forgive me, but I have to leave this meeting now. I cannot tolerate such an attitude,'" he said.
Anwar said this in his speech at the national-level Teachers' Day celebration at a hotel here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Prime Minister's wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Anwar said the situation has since changed significantly, with Chinese independent schools now showing greater alignment with the national education framework.
"That was 40 years ago. Today, all representatives of the associations speak Bahasa Melayu.
"More than 80 per cent of students in Chinese schools now sit for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination," he said.
He said the cabinet had agreed to allow students from UEC schools, tahfiz institutions, Arabic schools and international schools to pursue selected university courses, subject to conditions involving Bahasa Melayu and History.
He said students would be required to pass, or obtain at least a credit, in both subjects to qualify for university entry.
"When we approved this policy, we set a condition that they must pass or obtain at least a credit in Bahasa Melayu and History in order to qualify for university admission. This applies to everyone," he said.
Anwar said the same conditions would also apply to students from international schools, including Malaysians studying there, as well as those from tahfiz and Arabic schools.
He added that the issue should not be politicised, saying the government's position was aimed at safeguarding the national language and ensuring Malaysians understand the country's history and constitutional foundations.
"In life, we must have a spirit of compromise. But the one thing we cannot compromise on is language. Anyone living in Malaysia must have a firm command of the national language.
"To become a Malaysian citizen, one must understand our history, the Federal Constitution, the history of independence and our struggles against colonial powers," he said.
Anwar added that eligible students would not be limited to language-related university programmes, but could also pursue courses such as accounting and others.
However, he said the final decision on professional accreditation for certain programmes would rest with universities and the relevant professional bodies.
On Thursday, the cabinet approved new admission pathways to public universities for students outside the national education system, including those from tahfiz institutions, private schools and Chinese independent high schools.
The Higher Education Ministry said
the decision
reflects its commitment to ensuring inclusive and quality access to higher education for all qualified students, in line with the National Education Philosophy. (hyperlink: )
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mara to offer cadet pilot scholarships for STEM students, with priority for tahfiz school leavers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will soon open applications for its cadet pilot programme, offering financial sponsorship to qualified students to undergo flight training at selected academies.
"Mara will release the official advertisement along with the eligibility requirements for this cadet pilot programme very soon," Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said in a Facebook post.
Meanwhile, the minimum application requirements will include candidates from the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) stream.
However, priority will be given to tahfiz students from the STEM stream, though the application remains open to all candidates who meet the set criteria.
"The final phase of the interview will be conducted by me personally, where I will run the Quranic memorisation test based on the tasmik method," he added.
Quran tasmik is a crucial recitation-validation process used in hifz (Quranic memorisation), where a student reads newly memorised verses from memory to a teacher to correct errors in tajweed (pronunciation), fluency, and retention.
Among the flight training academies expected to be involved are the Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) and BATS Aviation, both of which have collaborations with commercial airlines to ensure the employability of graduates.
Asyraf added that academies receiving Mara sponsorship must have existing agreements with airlines to guarantee employment opportunities for trainees upon completion of their course.
"Academies sponsored by Mara must have agreements with airlines to secure jobs for the trainees who will be receiving this financial funding," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man in critical condition after shark attack in western Australia
Article:
SYDNEY: A man in his thirties was in a "critical" condition Saturday after being attacked by a shark in western Australia, rescue services told AFP.
The incident took place after 10am (0200 GMT) off Rottnest Island near the city of Perth, a spokesperson for St John Ambulance Western Australia said.
"The rescue helicopter is on scene, along with police and island emergency services," they added.
There have been nearly 1,300 shark incidents around Australia since 1791, of which more than 260 resulted in death, according to a database of the predators' encounters with humans.
The most recent fatality was a 12-year-old boy who died after being attacked by a shark in Sydney Harbour in January.
That attack was one of four recorded in the span of two days, which prompted officials to close dozens of the city's beaches.
It was the city's third recent shark-related death.
Australian scientists believe increasingly crowded waters and rising ocean temperatures are swaying sharks' migratory patterns, which may be contributing to a rise in attacks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: How a Philippine war crimes suspect thwarted arrest, for now
Article:
IT unfolded like a crime thriller: a fugitive making his escape under cover of darkness in the confused aftermath of a hail of gunfire.
Philippine Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for his alleged role in the country's deadly drug war, fled into the Manila night hours after an unprecedented shootout on Wednesday in the Senate where he had spent days holed up evading arrest.
The chaos involved different government forces: Senate security led by an official with ties to former President Rodrigo Duterte's camp and the National Bureau of Investigation overseen by an appointee of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
It was the second run-in involving dela Rosa in a week, after the senator escaped NBI agents in a cat-and-mouse chase.
The political drama has engulfed the archipelago for months, pitting the ruling Marcos against the camp of Duterte's daughter and current Vice-President Sara Duterte as the ICC pursues accountability for a drug crackdown that killed thousands.
Dela Rosa, a 64-year-old former police chief, is unrepentant about his role in the drug war and previously said he would happily accompany Duterte to The Hague where the former leader faces a trial for crimes against humanity.
On Monday, dela Rosa emerged from months of hiding to cast a crucial vote in a Senate leadership election expected to help shield the vice-president from a pending impeachment trial.
The government, which denies sending forces to arrest dela Rosa during Wednesday's chaos, says it is investigating whether the shooting was staged to allow his escape. Nobody was harmed.
"Gunshots in a national assembly are unheard of. It points to a deep crisis in the Philippines linked to the ICC issue," Duterte's former lawyer Nicholas Kaufman told Reuters.
The drama began in the late afternoon on Wednesday as rumours began to swirl that the NBI were on their way.
The chamber was under new leadership: dela Rosa's surprise arrival on Monday had allowed a pro-Duterte bloc of senators to seize a majority and install a new president, Alan Peter Cayetano.
Cayetano said he had driven his fugitive colleague into the building by car. CCTV footage shows the senator stumbling up a staircase, pursued by NBI agents.
He pushed a female agent, said former senator Antonio Trillanes, who was present and accompanied the NBI to serve the warrant. The NBI was tasked with arresting dela Rosa because of his residual influence over the police, Trillanes said.
[[nid:1440756]]
An anxious dela Rosa broadcast on Facebook appealing for supporters including "fellow men in uniform" to mobilise, saying government agents were about to arrest him. "Let us not allow another Filipino to be taken to The Hague," he said.
At around 7 pm, the head of Senate security Mao Aplasca, a retired police general and old friend of dela Rosa, announced to reporters his security team was "going to arrest someone" and the building would go into "total lockdown".
Aplasca said later he was referring to arresting NBI agents.
As the building went into lockdown on Wednesday, Aplasca and the Senate security began putting on ballistic vests.
They were not alone: Reuters reporters saw police and Philippine marines with rifles and Kevlar helmets, summoned by Aplasca, the military said later.
Aplasca led the armed men to an entrance to a neighbouring building, the headquarters of the Government Service Insurance System.
Ten minutes later, a volley of gunfire erupted – dozens of shots triggered, Aplasca said later, by NBI agents who raised their rifles.
His team responded with 27 "warning" shots, he said.
Reporters and Senate staff ran for cover, Reuters journalists among them, as Senate security flicked the lights in the corridor off, some continuing to broadcast live.
"The Senate is under attack! Pls help us," dela Rosa posted on Facebook.
Several floors above, despite the turmoil, House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil managed to file the impeachment papers, paving the way for the vice-president's trial.
By 8.30 pm on Wednesday, the drama was all over. The chief of police and Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla arrived and reporters were asked to leave.
Remulla said dela Rosa was "resting" in his office.
Senate security records produced by Cayetano showed he left the building at around 2.30 am - unchallenged by guards.
The drama over dela Rosa is unfinished: Justice Secretary Fredderick Vida said on Friday the Philippines would "definitely" comply with the ICC's arrest request and stop him from fleeing abroad.
Dela Rosa has filed an emergency appeal at the Supreme Court arguing the ICC lacks jurisdiction.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration rounds up 54 illegal immigrants in Sabah crackdown
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Immigration Department rounded up 54 foreigners for various immigration offences during enforcement operations in Sipitang and Semporna recently.
The foreigners were detained when the department carried out operations codenamed Op Sapu and Op Kutip, a crackdown against undocumented migrants in Sabah.
In Sipitang, the department inspected 68 people, resulting in the arrest of 50 of them under Op Sapu.
"They are suspected of various offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63, including lacking valid identification documents and violating pass conditions.
"Among those detained were 48 Filipinos. They comprised 14 men, 12 women, 10 boys and 12 girls.
"Also detained during the operation were two Pakistani men," the department said in a Facebook post.
Meanwhile, in Semporna, four Pakistani men were detained following inspections on six people during simultaneous Op Sapu and Op Kutip operations.
"We will continue to intensify enforcement operations to eradicate the presence of illegal immigrants and ensure that the country's immigration laws are strictly upheld," the department said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Bukit Aman probing claims of Jho Low's 'secret meetings' in Malaysia [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating reports that fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, returned to Malaysia for a "secret meeting" linked to 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB).
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said police had yet to verify the claims.
"We will investigate whether the meeting actually took place," he said after attending the police's Autism Day celebration at the Police Training Centre.
"We will issue a statement on any developments," he said when asked to comment on reports about the purported meeting.
Sarawak Report recently claimed that Low, who has been on the run for years, attended secret meetings in Kuala Lumpur last year linked to efforts to resolve 1MDB debts involving China.
On May 13, Khalid said
police investigations
into the fugitive businessman were continuing despite reports that he had sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump.
The Wall Street Journal recently reported that Low had allegedly sought assistance linked to Trump in an attempt to obtain relief from legal action in the United States.
Low remains wanted by authorities in several countries over his alleged role in the 1MDB scandal.
The WSJ report quoted a White House official as saying
Low's request
was not currently on its radar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Beckham becomes first British billionaire sportsman
Article:
LONDON: Former Manchester United F.C. and England star David Beckham has become Britain's first billionaire sportsman, according to the 2026 Sunday Times Rich List.
Beckham and his wife Victoria Beckham's collective wealth reached an estimated £1.185 billion (US$1.583 billion) this year, the Rich List compilers said.
That moved them into second place in the list of the UK's wealthiest sportspeople, behind the family of former Bernie Ecclestone chief executive, whose wealth was valued at £2 billion.
Beckham, who retired from playing in 2013, is a co-owner of Inter Miami CF, estimated to be Major League Soccer's most valuable club at £1.07 billion.
The 51-year-old also has lucrative brand ambassador roles for companies including Adidas and Hugo Boss.
Beckham captained England and won the Premier League and Champions League during a glittering career with United, before spells at Real Madrid CF, LA Galaxy, AC Milan and Paris Saint-Germain F.C..
Victoria Beckham's wealth has largely been generated from her fashion label after she originally found fame as a member of pop band Spice Girls.
Joining Beckham on the Rich List, seven-time Formula One champion Lewis Hamilton is fifth on the list, having built a fortune the Rich List calculated at £435 million.
Reigning Masters golf champion Rory McIlroy is seventh with a £325 million valuation.
Boxer Anthony Joshua is placed eighth with a fortune of £240 million, one place above his heavyweight rival Tyson Fury, who is ninth on £162 million.
FC Bayern Munich and England striker Harry Kane and retired former Wimbledon champion Andy Murray are joint 10th with £110 million each.
Among businesspeople with sporting associations, Manchester United F.C. co-owner Jim Ratcliffe has dropped down the overall Rich List.
His fortune shrank by £1.85 billion, according to the list compilers, to £15.194 billion.
The list compilers lowered the value of INEOS to £17 billion owing to "rising debt, falling revenues and a loss of £515.7 million".
Promoters Barry Hearn and Eddie Hearn have joined Britain's billionaire club, with their combined wealth estimated at £1.035 billion.
Barry is the founder and president of Matchroom Sport, one of the leading promoters in boxing, darts and snooker, while his son Eddie is chairman of the organisation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Starbucks to lay off hundreds of employees in US
Article:
ISTANBUL: Starbucks announced on Friday that it will lay off 300 employees in the United States (US), around 3 per cent of its labour force in the country, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing FOX.
"We are taking further action under the Back to Starbucks strategy, building on our strong business momentum and working to return the company to durable, profitable growth," the coffee chain said in a statement to FOX.
It stated that leaders have taken a "hard look" at their respective functions to further sharpen focus, prioritise work, reduce complexity and lower costs.
"As a result, we're eliminating approximately 300 US support roles," it noted.
Starbucks is also reviewing its international support organisation as it focus on being a "world-class licensor" and expect additional role impacts outside the US, according to the statement.
The company is also closing some regional support offices. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teachers architects of future generation, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The role of teachers has become more critical than ever in shaping a generation with wisdom, strong morals and values amid increasingly complex global challenges, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said a teacher's duty and responsibility is more than just educating their students, but also building their confidence and nurturing them to uphold dignity.
In a post on his Facebook page in conjunction with Teachers' Day today, Anwar recalled his experience as an educator when he formed Yayasan Anda Akademik in the early 1970s.
"At that time, I saw many young people from underprivileged families who had dropped out of education, arriving with anxiety about their future.
"That experience taught me that a teacher's duty is not just to impart knowledge, but to build confidence, uphold human dignity, and instil hope and ambition in life.
"As global challenges become increasingly complex, the role of teachers is more critical than ever in shaping a generation that possesses the moral strength and soul to infuse knowledge with human values and wisdom, in line with the Madani vision," he said.
"Without values and adab (etiquette), development will only result in progress that has lost its direction," he said.
Anwar also described teachers as the backbone of nation-building and the architects of the country's morality, values, and civilisation.
"We recognise the service, sacrifice, and dedication of teachers.
"May all educators continue to be blessed with strength, resilience, and commitment in educating our children for a Malaysia that is more advanced, prosperous, and sustainable.
"Happy Teachers' Day - Guru Bitara Nadi Negara."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Married couple arrested in disabled man's murder probe
Article:
KOTA BHARU: Police arrested a married couple yesterday evening to assist in the investigation into the murder of a disabled man in Padang Lati, Bachok, in April.
The 26-year-old male suspect was remanded for seven days today, while his wife, 27, was remanded for five days.
Bachok police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin said the suspects were arrested at a house in Kampung Padang Salim at 4.30pm yesterday.
Urine tests on both suspects were negative, though the husband has a prior criminal record.
"The couple are friends of the victim," Mohamad Ismail said, adding that investigations were ongoing.
The investigation into the murder of Muhammad Zakir Fikry Mohd Zainal, 20, is being carried out under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
The offence carries the death penalty or imprisonment for 30 to 40 years with at least 12 strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
Zakir, who had a learning disability, died after he was believed to have been beaten.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man charged with death threat to woman in Kota Kinabalu court
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — A 21-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here with threatening to cause death to a woman.
Muhammad Wahyu Abdullah pleaded not guilty before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus to criminally intimidating one Sitti Aminah by threatening to cause death at a rented room on the first floor of Kampung Air here at about 12.30pm on May 5, this year.
The charge under Section 506 of the Penal Code provides for a jail term of up to seven years or a fine or both, on conviction.
The court set July 9 for pre-trial case management and released the accused on bail of RM1,200 in two sureties.
Meanwhile, a 41-year-old man was fined RM2,800 or six months’ jail for keeping a stolen wall fan.
Maarof Garib pleaded guilty to having the stolen wall fan belonging to Shell Station Wisma Innoprise, Jalan UMS, at the male surau of the petrol station at about 12.30pm on May 9.
The offence under Section 411 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of up to five years, or a fine, or both, on conviction.
The court heard from the prosecution that the complainant discovered the wall fan missing during a patrol at the said surau at about 11am on May 7, before lodging a police report.
Police later arrested Maarof at a house in Kg Delima, Manggatal, in connection with the case and seized the stolen wall fan from him.
The complainant also identified the seized fan as belonging to the Shell station.
Inspector Mohamad Hamidi Mohamad Hamzah prosecuted. — Daily Express

Title: Driver loses control, crashes car into Kuching coffee shop, two sent to hospital
Article:
KUCHING, May 16 — An elderly man lost control of his sedan and crashed into a coffee shop near Jalan Rock traffic light junction here today.
See Hua Daily News
reported that the incident occurred at around 11am, and the elderly driver was unharmed.
Personnel from the Civil Defence Force responded to the incident and arrived at the scene to provide assistance
It was also learnt that two individuals from the coffeeshop were sent to hospital by ambulance, possibly due to shock from the incident.
No other casualties were reported at the scene. — The Borneo Post

Title: PKR sec-gen says Pasir Gudang MP does not speak for govt in resignation call over UEC varsity decision
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 16 — PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh today distanced the government from Pasir Gudang MP Hassan Abdul Karim’s call for two ministers to resign over issues linked to the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC).
She said Hassan’s remarks were his personal views and did not represent the government’s position.
“The decision to allow Chinese Private Secondary Schools graduates or UEC holders to follow certain programmes at public higher education institutions was made with clear conditions, including having SPM qualifications and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and history subjects,” she told reporters after attending a PKR programme at a hotel here.
Fuziah said the issue was not new, noting that previous administrations had also imposed similar conditions to allow UEC holders to pursue studies at public higher education institutions.
“This matter should not be made into a tirade because it is clear and has also been implemented before,” she said.
Yesterday, Hassan called on Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir to resign, claiming they had failed to defend the national education policy involving public universities.
The Higher Education Ministry had earlier announced that graduates from tahfiz schools, Chinese independent secondary schools and other institutions outside the national education system would be allowed to pursue selected programmes at public higher education institutions, subject to conditions.
The move followed a Cabinet decision on admission pathways for students outside the national education system into public higher education institutions.

Title: Anwar: Educators must accept criticism positively and show resilience in facing challenges
Article:
IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said educators need to be big-hearted in accepting challenges and criticism positively, especially regarding shortcomings and problems involving students.
He said education cannot be separated from being linked to personal, character, human or humanity and any problems that arise are often linked to schools until they sometimes burden educators.
“Social problems certainly affect children in schools and certainly cause commotion. It seems like this only happens in schools, when what happens sometimes is shortcomings, moral corruption, that’s because we are part of society.
“But I hope teachers see this positively. So that’s why if you are burdened with unfair criticism, I hope teachers would accept it well to raise up and ensure the next generation is much better than us,” he said.
He was launching the 55th National Teachers’ Day Celebration at Casuarina Hotel here, today, which was also attended by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.
Anwar, who sometimes refers to himself as ‘Cikgu Anwar’, said the Ministry of Education (KPM) is currently preparing an education framework that not only focuses on economic and technological progress such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and digital, but also maintains its identity and noble values.
“If we look at almost all countries, they have their education policies and are evaluated from time to time. But in Malaysia, in this Madani country, there is something different. We don’t want to be left behind in terms of new technology, AI. Children must be prepared for current needs, otherwise we will be left behind.
“But in moving towards all these goals, we must never ignore or underestimate the importance of defending our human values as Malaysians and the values referred to as the morals of society,” he said.
In addition, he emphasised the concept of “Excellence Without a Soul” by emphasising that academic intelligence alone is not enough if it is separated from human values, morals and social concerns.
Meanwhile, the Prime Minister also shared a personal story about his close relationship with his former teacher who is also the former Education director-general, the late Tan Sri Rahman Arshad, as a symbol of the high respect a student has for teachers.
At the ceremony, Anwar also presented three awards, namely the Best School in the World, namely Sekolah Kebangsaan Putrajaya Presint 11 (1), Putrajaya, the National Education Leadership Award to Dr. Ahmad Rafee Che Kassim and the National Teacher Award to Abdul Rasid Bakar. — Bernama

Title: Selangor launches Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa to expand support for PwDs, says state exco
Article:
SHAH ALAM, May 16 — The Selangor government today launched the Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa Selangor (BRAIS) 2026 programme to expand existing support for the welfare of persons with disabilities (PwDs).
State Women Development and Social Welfare Committee chairman Anfaal Saari said the initiative, under the Yayasan Anak Istimewa Selangor (YANIS), is a rebranding of the Bantuan Khas Anak Istimewa Selangor (ANIS) aimed at delivering more comprehensive and high-impact development for PwDs in the state.
She said BRAIS 2026 introduces several significant improvements, including a new category of assistance for disability-friendly residential toilet facilities with aid of RM1,500, as well as assistance of RM1,200 for medication, supplements and other special needs.
“The additions are intended to ensure that facilities and basic healthcare needs for PwDs at home are better maintained and more comfortable.
“Additionally, the age limit for beneficiaries has been increased from 18 to 60 years, enabling adult PwDs to access assistance for equipment, medical needs and therapy support,” she said at a press conference after the launch of BRAIS 2026 and the HajiKids 2.0 programme here today.
Elaborating further, Anfaal said that in addition to the new categories, BRAIS 2026 will retain existing support, including assistance for therapy centre fees (RM1,200), special equipment (up to RM3,000) and special education school fees (RM120).
She added that the submission of supporting documents for applications under the initiative will be conducted entirely online to expedite the approval process and ease matters for parents and guardians.
Applications for BRAIS 2026 will open from June 1 at 8.30 am until 5 pm on June 22 via the official website at www.yanis.org.my/brais.
In another development, Anfaal said a total of 2,500 children aged between four and 12 were celebrated today through the HajiKids 2.0 programme, a unique Islamic educational initiative that uses practical simulation methods and interactive visual technology to provide children with an immersive learning experience on the haj pilgrimage.
She said HajiKids 2.0 is also seen as an excellent collaborative platform in the state government’s efforts to mainstream PwDs in Selangor.
“This spirit of inclusivity is clearly reflected through sponsorship by YANIS for the participation of 35 special needs children from the YANIS Centre, as well as 18 participants with cerebral palsy in the programme,” she said.
In addition, Anfaal said YANIS aims to explore long-term strategic collaboration with leading animation company Warnakala Studios to leverage popular digital ecosystems such as Didi & Friends as a medium to enhance public awareness through more disability-friendly communication. — Bernama

Title: Overnight queues, RM50 runners and shattered hopes at KL Audemars Piguet x Swatch launch (VIDEO)
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — By the time the sun rose over Mid Valley Megamall this morning, Ayu Omar and her daughter Allesya had already spent nearly 10 hours waiting for a watch from the new Audemars Piguet x Swatch “Royal Pop” collection.
The 46-year-old administrator from Banting, Selangor arrived at the mall at around 9.30 last night, armed with little more than a camping chair and her daughter’s laptop, which Allesya used to complete an assignment while sitting on the chair outside the Swatch boutique.
The pair were among dozens who camped overnight for the worldwide launch of the collection, a collaboration that drew crowds long before the mall’s doors officially opened at 10am.
Overnight queue for limited drop
“Firstly, I like Swatch. Secondly, it’s because of the collaboration with AP,” Ayu said when approached while seated cross-legged near the North Court entrance.
“AP is a huge name in the watch industry, so when they combined Swatch with AP, I felt it was really worth getting, especially at quite an affordable price.”
Like many others in line, Ayu’s night was repeatedly interrupted by confusion over crowd control.
At first, customers queued inside the mall before being instructed to leave. They later waited outside through the night before being allowed back in around 7.30am, three people at a time.
By 8.15am, the queue had already snaked several metres, ending just before the centre court.
Limited stock, rising tension
Then came the announcement that shattered Ayu’s hopes.
At around 9.15am, a Swatch staff member informed the crowd that only a limited number of watches were available.
Although the “Royal Pop” collection was not officially a numbered limited edition, there would not be enough units for everyone in line today.
The mood shifted almost immediately.
Those seated on the floor scrambled to the front to check their chances, while others remained frozen in place, staring towards the boutique entrance.
Only around 70 people appeared to remain within the estimated cut-off point.
Ayu was not one of them.
After lingering for a while longer, she quietly gave up and left.
Ayu Omar, 46 (centre, wearing hijab) after the announcement of the store will sell limited number of units on May 16, 2026. — Picture by Yusof Isa
“I’ve liked Swatch since school,” she said earlier, estimating she had followed the brand for more than 20 years.
“But when you’re a collector, even if you can’t wear it on your wrist, that’s okay. You still want it as part of your collection because it’s very different.”
RM50 runners fuel resale scramble
The frenzy surrounding the release also revealed a parallel economy quietly operating within the queue itself.
One man, who declined to be named, admitted he and his friends had hired 14 “runners” to queue on their behalf, paying each person RM50.
Most of the runners were easily identifiable by their attire – black T-shirts, jeans and slippers – with many appearing uninterested in the watches themselves.
The man refused to disclose how much his customers paid for the service, though checks by
Malay Mail
on social media platforms such as Threads found queueing services advertising prices ranging from RM600 to over RM1,000 above the retail price of the watch.
“When there’s a willing buyer, there’s a willing seller,” the man said.
Malay Mail
also observed several people passing around debit and credit cards to other people waiting in line after exiting the store with their purchases.
Swatch X Audemars Piguet Royal Pop watches model release displayed in presentation case at Mid Valley Megamall in Kuala Lumpur on May 16, 2026. — Picture by Yusof Isa
Swatch stores are enforcing a one-watch-per-person, per-store, policy for the release, though some customers questioned whether the restrictions were enough to deter scalpers.
‘Just here for the hype’
Among them was Ammanjit Singh, a 39-year-old banker from Sri Gombak, who just walked out of the store after purchasing the watch, carrying bags of crisps inside a carry-on bag after spending the night outside the mall.
“I work a nine-to-five job. I haven’t slept since yesterday,” he said.
Ammanjit readily admitted he is not a hardcore collector and was there largely for the atmosphere.
“I’m just here for the hype. My friend is the real, proper watch enthusiast,” he said.
Still, even Ammanjit noticed how organised some of the resellers appeared to be.
“I do feel bad for people who genuinely wanted the watch, but these scalpers, there’s not much you can do. They queued up too,” he said.
